mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration that triggers immediate equity sell-offs, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns remain positive in over 55% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.4pp, or the signal yields a negative Sharpe ratio in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength that sustains corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce positive 60-day forward returns in more than 40% of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during periods of low inflation reflects healthy capacity utilization without pricing pressure, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 20-day returns when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 3% annually, or the mechanism predictive power vanishes outside the specified low-inflation regime.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly increases corporate revenue expectations, creating immediate upward pressure on equity valuations and yielding positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 growth shows zero or negative correlation with 5-day forward returns across multiple market cycles, or the signal information coefficient remains below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate level exceeding 2.5% triggers financial condition tightening that raises equity discount rates and compresses valuation multiples, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns remain positive in over 60% of periods where FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.5%, or the threshold effect disappears when controlling for earnings growth.

mechanism_narrative: Strong nonfarm payroll growth combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor demand that supports consumer spending and corporate profits, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE produces negative 20-day forward returns in more than 45% of observations, or the interaction term fails to improve model fit over single predictors.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden contraction in industrial production during a high-inflation environment signals stagflationary supply shocks that force immediate equity repricing, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during high inflation periods fail to predict negative 5-day returns, or the mechanism shows no predictive edge when CPIAUCSL growth is below 4%.
